deep_dive.title: Sampling-Based Coordination-Informed Multi-Objective Multi-Robot Reinforcement Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-robot systems must simultaneously optimize competing objectives while maintaining coordinated behavior. Existing multi-agent reinforcement learning approaches often rely on fixed or centralized coordination, which limits adaptability and violates distributed constraints. This work introduces the Coordination-Informed Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning (CIMORL) framework, integrating a distributed weight prediction mechanism, a privileged expert training strategy, and theoretical guarantees for Pareto-optimal solutions. We present the base CIMORL method alongside two sampling-based variants, CIMORL-TS (Tree Search) and CIMORL-MPPI (MPPI), which leverage privileged global information during training to enable fully decentralized deployment. Experimental validation in cooperative and adversarial scenarios demonstrates a $21.2\%$ hypervolume improvement and superior policy stability compared to state-of-the-art baselines. Real-world experiments with Crazyflie drones further validate the framework's robustness in resource allocation and multi-attacker multi-defend scenarios under partial observability.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: centralized training with decentralized execution for multi-robot multi-objective RL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向多机器人多目标强化学习的集中训练分散执行
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向多机器人多目标强化学习的集中训练分散执行
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向多机器人多目标强化学习的集中训练分散执行
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CIMORL addresses decentralized multi-robot multi-objective RL by integrating distributed weight prediction, privileged expert training, and sampling-based variants (tree search, MPPI) to achieve Pareto-optimal solutions with fully decentralized execution.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 多机器人系统需同时优化多个竞争目标并保持协调，现有方法依赖固定或集中式协调。本文提出CIMORL框架，通过分布式权重预测和特权专家训练实现集中训练分散执行，并引入基于采样的CIMORL-TS和CIMORL-MPPI变体。理论保证帕累托最优，实验表明该方法在完全分散执行下高效且适应性强。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 多机器人系统需同时优化多个竞争目标并保持协调，现有方法依赖固定或集中式协调。本文提出CIMORL框架，通过分布式权重预测和特权专家训练实现集中训练分散执行，并引入基于采样的CIMORL-TS和CIMORL-MPPI变体。理论保证帕累托最优，实验表明该方法在完全分散执行下高效且适应性强。

deep_dive.title: HyPOLE: Hyperproperty-Guided Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning under Partial Observation
deep_dive.abstract: Formal specification is a powerful tool to guide the learning process and provides significant advantages over reward shaping: (1) mathematical rigor; (2) expressiveness to specify objectives and constraints, and (3) the ability to define tactics to achieve objectives. However, these benefits remain largely unexplored in the context of Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL). This paper introduces HyPOLE, a novel framework for MARL under partial observability, where learning is guided by the expressive power of the so-called hyperproperties and, in particular, the temporal logic HyperLTL. We integrate Centralized Training for Decentralized Execution (CTDE) techniques with HyPOLE to synthesize decentralized policies, and our evaluation on SMAC, MessySMAC, and WildFire benchmark demonstrates clear advantages over baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体强化学习结合中心化训练与去中心化执行
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多智能体强化学习结合中心化训练与去中心化执行
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多智能体强化学习结合中心化训练与去中心化执行
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: HyPOLE integrates CTDE with hyperproperty-guided learning for decentralized policies under partial observability, addressing sparse reward and credit assignment in MARL.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有MARL方法缺乏利用形式化规约指导学习的能力。本文提出HyPOLE框架，将超属性与中心化训练去中心化执行（CTDE）结合，在部分观测下合成去中心化策略。实验表明该方法能有效提升合作与协调效率，为形式化方法在MARL中的应用开辟新途径。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有MARL方法缺乏利用形式化规约指导学习的能力。本文提出HyPOLE框架，将超属性与中心化训练去中心化执行（CTDE）结合，在部分观测下合成去中心化策略。实验表明该方法能有效提升合作与协调效率，为形式化方法在MARL中的应用开辟新途径。

deep_dive.title: Smart charging of large fleets of Electric Vehicles: Independent Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning approaches
deep_dive.abstract: The electrification of transportation through electric vehicles introduces new challenges for power grid management, such as increased peak demand, voltage fluctuations, line overloads, and the integration of variable renewable energy sources. To enable efficient integration of EVs while minimizing costs for users and avoiding network overloads, implicit coordination between EVs is required. This work compares two independent multi-agent reinforcement learning approaches for optimizing such decentralized EV charging: contextual combinatorial bandits and policy gradient algorithms. Using a realistic simulation environment with autonomous agents making decisions based on local environmental information (including price signals, state-of-charge, and temporal constraints), we evaluate their performance across varying congestion levels, and mixed-strategy configurations with heterogeneous agent groups under dynamic electricity pricing derived from real photovoltaic production data.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: decentralized multi-agent RL for EV charging coordination
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向电动汽车充电协调的分散式多智能体强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向电动汽车充电协调的分散式多智能体强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向电动汽车充电协调的分散式多智能体强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Comparing contextual combinatorial bandits and policy gradient for multi-agent EV charging, this paper demonstrates effective decentralized coordination that reduces costs and prevents grid overload in realistic simulations.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 大规模电动汽车充电面临电网管理挑战，需隐式协调。本文对比了上下文组合赌博机和策略梯度两种独立多智能体强化学习方法，在真实模拟环境中验证了其降低用户成本、避免网络过载的有效性，为分散式电动汽车充电协调提供了实用基准。

quick_skim.title: Training Observable Control Policies to Expose Agent State Through Actions
quick_skim.abstract: Physical or operational constraints often impose communications limitations on autonomous agents. Such limitations complicate monitoring or multiagent coordination. Even when strong communications are absent, some information may still be available. The remainder of the relevant agent state may be reconstructed via estimation. The actions taken by an agent are a potential source of information -- as the agent interacts with the environment, these actions may be observed even in the absence of explicit communication. We investigate using actions to estimate the state of an agent, using reinforcement learning to develop policies which make the estimation problem more tractable. Policy observability is encouraged through the training reward and is analyzed using simulation of the trained agent. In an aircraft tracking problem a policy with enhanced observability is found that has minimal impact on nominal task performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multiagent coordination and state estimation via actions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过动作进行多智能体协调与状态估计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过动作进行多智能体协调与状态估计
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过动作进行多智能体协调与状态估计
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Uses RL to train policies that expose agent state through actions, enabling decentralized state estimation in multiagent systems with communication constraints.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在多智能体协作中，通信限制常导致状态估计困难。本文利用强化学习训练可观测控制策略，使智能体的动作能暴露其内部状态，从而在无显式通信条件下实现去中心化状态估计。实验证明该方法能有效提升多智能体协调性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在多智能体协作中，通信限制常导致状态估计困难。本文利用强化学习训练可观测控制策略，使智能体的动作能暴露其内部状态，从而在无显式通信条件下实现去中心化状态估计。实验证明该方法能有效提升多智能体协调性能。

quick_skim.title: Towards Value-Constrained Credit Assignment in Fully Delegated AI Cooperatives
quick_skim.abstract: We propose a framework for reward allocation in fully delegated AI cooperatives where humans are represented by agents that contribute data and participate in model updates under heterogeneous value constraints. The key idea is to credit only those updates that remain admissible after screening them against each principal's value profile. We formulate value-conditioned gradient filtering, online marginal contribution signals, and cumulative revenue settlement within a traversal learning (TL) substrate. TL is especially attractive here because it performs decentralized backpropagation without the quality loss associated with aggregation-centric distributed learning and, we argue, offers a finer attribution substrate than FedAvg-style federated learning by preserving explicit traversal and gradient paths. The framework is positioned against data valuation, federated contribution estimation, personalized federated learning, and pluralistic alignment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent credit assignment with decentralized backpropagation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体信用分配与去中心化反向传播
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体信用分配与去中心化反向传播
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体信用分配与去中心化反向传播
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes value-constrained credit assignment for AI cooperatives using decentralized traversal learning, enabling multi-agent reward allocation under heterogeneous constraints.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对全委托AI合作中的信用分配问题，提出了一种基于遍历学习的价值约束信用分配框架。该方法通过价值条件梯度过滤和在线边际贡献信号，实现去中心化的反向传播和奖励分配。实验表明该框架在保证各主体价值约束的同时，能高效分配信用，为多智能体系统中的去中心化训练提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对全委托AI合作中的信用分配问题，提出了一种基于遍历学习的价值约束信用分配框架。该方法通过价值条件梯度过滤和在线边际贡献信号，实现去中心化的反向传播和奖励分配。实验表明该框架在保证各主体价值约束的同时，能高效分配信用，为多智能体系统中的去中心化训练提供了新思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 多智能体强化学习, especially work that connects or combines: multi-agent reinforcement learning; cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning; decentralized multi-agent reinforcement learning; centralized training for multi-agent reinforcement learning; decentralized execution in multi-agent systems; Markov games in multi-agent reinforcement learning; multi agent reinforcement learning algorithms; cooperative multi agent reinforcement learning applications; decentralized multi agent reinforcement learning methods; comparison of value decomposition and actor critic methods.

quick_skim.title: LLawCo: Learning Laws of Cooperation for Modeling Embodied Multi-Agent Behavior
quick_skim.abstract: Embodied agents operating in decentralized and partially observable environments have attracted growing attention in recent years. However, existing large language model (LLM)-based agents often exhibit behaviors that are misaligned with their partners or inconsistent with the environment state, leading to inefficient cooperation and poor task success. To address this challenge, we propose a novel framework, Learning Laws of Cooperation (LLawCo), that enables embodied agents to autonomously align with both their partners and task objectives. Our framework allows agents to reflect on past failures to extract misaligned behavioral patterns, which are used to derive high-level behavioral laws, such as "Talk when necessary" and "Wait for partner." These laws are explicitly incorporated into the agents' chains of thought via supervised fine-tuning, aligning their reasoning with task requirements and the behavior of other agents. To evaluate our approach, we introduce PARTNR-Dialog, a large-scale multi-agent communicative and cooperative planning benchmark built on the PARTNR environment. Experiments on existing tasks and our new benchmark demonstrate significant improvements in cooperative efficiency and task success rates. Across four backbone LLMs, our method achieves average success rate improvements of 4.5% on the PARTNR-Dialog benchmark and 6.8% on the TDW-MAT benchmark over state-of-the-art open-source communicative agent frameworks. See the LLawCo project page for details: https://www.merl.com/research/highlights/LLawCo
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cooperative embodied multi-agent behavior with LLM-based reflection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM反射的协作具身多智能体行为
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于LLM反射的协作具身多智能体行为
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM反射的协作具身多智能体行为
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LLawCo, a framework enabling embodied agents to autonomously align with partners and tasks by extracting misaligned patterns from failures to derive high-level behavioral laws.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 具身智能体在分散部分可观察环境中常出现行为不协调。本文提出LLawCo框架，通过让智能体反思失败提取失调模式，进而推导高层行为法则（如“必要时交谈”），实现自主协作对齐。实验表明该方法有效提升了协作任务成功率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 具身智能体在分散部分可观察环境中常出现行为不协调。本文提出LLawCo框架，通过让智能体反思失败提取失调模式，进而推导高层行为法则（如“必要时交谈”），实现自主协作对齐。实验表明该方法有效提升了协作任务成功率。
